titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L1692
GA L1692
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Available for noncommercial re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators and the subsequent derivative is licensed as ShareAlike (Creative Commons by-nc-sa)

projectDesc.p: This transcription was initially made by the Byzantine Text of John Project in 2004-7, led by Roderic L. Mullen, and later adopted for the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John.

The IGNTP transcription file was duplicated for the standalone version of the Byzantine Text of John on 9.7.2014. This means that there may be slight discrepancies between the 2007 file used to prepared the Byzantine apparatus and the 2014 transcription in the present file.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 W1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-Α number: L 1692 (Αthos, Dionysiou 587)Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 2r John begins folio 2, μονο κυριακεςpaper appendix supplies Jn 3.13-17 and 6.35-39 and 10.9-16 (Τype Lsel - Sundays and selected rdgs)Τranscribed by: RLΜΤranscription begun: 18.06.02Τranscription finished: 10.11.02Τransference to Collate by: RLΜΤransference to Collate begun: 18.06.02Τransference to Collate finished: 10.11.02 File date and time:Ρlaced in Level1: RLΜLevel1 file date and time: 10.11.02Ρlaced in Level2: read a second time and compared with the collation of Johannes Κaravidopoulos. Ηoly Week and Μenologion read in ΜuensterΡlaced in Level2 by: RLΜLevel2 file date and time: 03.12.02Τhese files were compared and differences resolved by:Ρlaced in Level3:Ρlaced in Level3 by:Final transcription file date and time: Page layout added by: acmStart date: 6.11.13 End date: 8.11.13Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes. Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No. Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No. 7.53 - 8.11 No. Other remarks: Supplement is added to the end of this manuscript.

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman
John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτoυργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)

ab.note: Gospel Resurrection 9 reading (John 20,19-31) is cross-referenced to the S1 W2 D1 reading.
The following leaves have been written by two different hands, see supplements.
